key_insight: On-policy distillation works mainly as an exploration catalyst: it guides students toward correct reasoning paths, but its success depends more on signal quality than on teacher scale or extra sampling.
engineering_relevance: For computational engineering workflows, this suggests distillation systems should prioritize robust, well-regulated guidance signals to improve reliable search and reasoning without assuming larger teachers will automatically yield better performance.
method: Analyzed OPD training dynamics in terms of role, pathologies, and regulation
Identified two failure modes: student-teacher mismatch and length exploitation
Tested lightweight signal regulations including advantage clipping and log-scale compression
evidence_signal: Across seven benchmarks, the regulated OPD variants reduced length exploitation and consistently outperformed prior OPD variants and RLVR baselines.
watch_item: If the teacher-student gap is too large or token-level signals are poorly scaled, OPD can misguide exploration or encourage degenerate length shortcuts.
paper_title: Demystifying On-Policy Distillation: Roles, Pathologies, and Regulations
